data.title: Citi names Gonzalo Luchetti new CFO, shuffles up U.S. Personal Banking unit
data.description:
Citigroup (C) said Tuesday that Gonzalo Luchetti will become the investment bank's new chief financial officer after Mark Mason transitions out of the role in March 2026. In addition, Citi announced several changes to its U.S. personal banking business.

When he leaves his CFO post, Mason will become executive vice chair of Citi (C) and senior executive advisor to Chair and CEO Jane Fraser, with responsibility for advising on strategic efforts, including preparing the company for investor day.

Luchetti has served as Citi’s (C) head of U.S. Personal Banking since 2021. He joined the lender in 2006.

As part of the bank's restructuring aimed at simplifying its business model, Citi (C) said its Retail Bank will be integrated into the Wealth business, with the U.S. Everyday Banking, Citi Priority, Citigold and Citigold Private Client relationship tiers consolidated into a single group under the leadership of Kate Luft, who will become head of U.S. Retail Banking and Citigold, reporting to Andy Sieg.

In addition, Citi's (C) Branded Cards and Retail Services businesses will be combined to form U.S. Consumer Cards. That business will be led by Pam Habner, who has served as head of Branded Cards and Lending for Citi since 2020. She will report to Fraser and become a member of the firm’s executive management team.

C shares slipped 0.1% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Zevra Therapeutics announces CFO transition
data.description:
• Zevra Therapeutics (NASDAQ:ZVRA) announced  on Thursday that its CFO and Treasurer, R. LaDuane Clifton, will be stepping down from his position, effective December 31, 2025.
  • The company has initiated a search to identify its next CFO.

data.title: Everest Group appoints new CFO
data.description:
• Everest Group (EG) named Elias Habayeb as its next EVP and Group chief financial officer, effective around May 1, 2026.
  • Habayeb replaces Mark Kociancic, who will retire after Q1 reporting and remain as a special advisor during the transition. Habayeb most recently served as CFO of Corebridge Financial (CRBG).

data.title: Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. GAAP EPS of $0.46 misses by $0.01, revenue of $257.4M beats by $4.98M
data.description:
• Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. press release (PXED): FQ4 GAAP EPS of $0.46 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $257.4M (+7.2% Y/Y) beats by $4.98M.

  • Business Outlook

    For fiscal year 2026, the Company expects revenue to be in the range of $1,025.0 million to $1,035.0 million. Adjusted EBITDA for the same period is expected to range between $244.0 million and $249.0 million.

  • FY Revenue consensus is $1.02B
